concepts.title: Witting reaction for carbonyls
concepts.conceptBody:
Witting Reaction

This method involves a convenient method of converting aldehydes and ketones into alkenes by using a special class of compounds called phosphorous yields also called wittig reagent. Primary or secondary alkyl halide is first treated with triphenyl phosphine the phosphonium halide produced in the above reaction is converted into phosphorane by adding a strong base like ${{\text{C}}_{6}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}\text{Li}$ or $\text{n}-{{\text{C}}_{4}}{{\text{H}}_{9}}-\text{Li}$, Phosphorane is stablised by resonance.

The Triphenyl group of phosphorane has a strong tendency to pull oxygen atom of the aldehyde or ketone forming alkene.

(R, R’, R” and R”’ may be hydrogen or any alkyl group).

concepts.title: Cleavage of ethers
concepts.conceptBody:
Cleavage of Ethers

Olefins can be formed by the treatment of ethers with very strong bases such as alkylsodium, alkylithium or sodamide.

The reaction is aided by electron withdrawing groups in the $\beta$-position.

For example ${{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}OC{{H}_{2}}CH{{(COO{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}})}_{2}}$ forms alkene just by heating without any base at all.

${{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}\text{OC}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{CH}{{\left( \text{COO}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}} \right)}_{2}}\xrightarrow[{}]{\Delta }\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}=\text{C}{{\left( \text{COO}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}} \right)}_{2}}+{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{5}}\text{OH}$

The mechanism probably involves a cyclic intermediate

examples.title: What is the product of the reaction between an aldehyde and a primary amine?

Which carbonyl compound would react violently with water?

An carbonyl compounds gives nuclrophilic addition reaction with
(1) $\mathrm{HCN}$
(2) $\mathrm{NaSHO}_3$
(3) $R-M g X$.
(4) $\mathrm{NH} 2-\mathrm{NH} 2$
